items.headline: Blue Jays ride an 8-run first inning to a 10-4 win over the Diamondbacks, snapping 4-game skid
items.description: — Kazuma Okamoto hit a solo homer and had three RBI, Nathan Lukes added a three-run double and the Toronto Blue Jays used an eight-run first inning to cruise past the Arizona Diamondbacks for a 10-4 victory Sunday that prevented a three-game sweep.

items.headline: Women in black confound Australia sevens stars again
items.description: Australia's rugby sevens women have suffered yet another final let-down against their great New Zealand rivals, missing out on the marquee Hong Kong Sevens crown with a 19-14 defeat.

items.headline: Moniak and Karros homer as the Rockies beat the Dodgers 9-6
items.description: — Mickey Moniak hit a go-ahead two-run homer in the seventh, and the Colorado Rockies held on to beat Los Angeles 9-6 on Sunday and give the Dodgers’ their first losing streak of the season.

items.headline: Stocks up, stocks down: The Coleman race is wide open; stars have stinkers
items.description: Last year's Coleman Medal winner reminded the comp he's still the No. 1 forward in the league, but one star free agent didn't do his contract negotiations any favours. Here's whose stocks are up and down after Round 6.

items.headline: Diamondbacks' Corbin Carroll leaves with low back tightness
items.description: Diamondbacks outfielder Corbin Carroll left Sunday's game against the Blue Jays before the fifth inning because of low back tightness.

items.headline: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 25 as reigning champion Thunder open playoffs with 119-84 win over Suns
items.description: — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 25 points and the Oklahoma City Thunder began their title defense with a 119-84 rout of the Phoenix Suns in Game 1 of their first-round Western Conference playoff series on Sunday.

items.headline: 2026 NBA playoffs: Western Conference first-round takeaways
items.description: Here's what has stood out from the latest playoff games of the Western Conference's first round.

items.headline: Dragons set to fire head coach Shane Flanagan
items.description: Shane Flanagan is set to be fired as coach of St George Illawarra following the Dragons' winless start to the NRL season.

items.headline: Tyler Reddick tops Kyle Larson in OT for 5th Cup Series win
items.description: Tyler Reddick became the fourth driver to win five of the first nine NASCAR Cup Series races in a season, and the first since Dale Earnhardt nearly four decades ago, when he passed Kyle Larson on the final lap of overtime at Kansas Speedway on Sunday.

items.headline: 2026 NBA playoffs: Schedule, scores, news and highlights
items.description: The Eastern and Western Conference playoff brackets are set after Sunday's games. See the full schedule of games.

items.headline: Wedgewood sharp in net, O'Connor scores 1st goal in more than a year as Avs beat Kings 2-1 in Game 1
items.description: — Logan O’Connor scored for the first time in more than a year, Scott Wedgewood stopped 24 shots in his first Stanley Cup playoffs start and the...

items.headline: Stewart Cink cards 63, wins Senior PGA Championship by 6
items.description: Stewart Cink closed with a 9-under 63 and pulled away from the field with an astonishing stretch for a six-shot victory in the Senior PGA Championship.

items.headline: TJ Friedl has 3-run double in 9th, and Reds rally for 7-4 win in 10 for 3-game sweep of Twins
items.description: — TJ Friedl hit a go-ahead, three-run double in the ninth inning, Rece Hinds added a two-run double in the 10th, and the Cincinnati Reds rallied past the Minnesota Twins 7-4 on Sunday for a three-game sweep.

items.headline: WWE WrestleMania 42 Night 2: Live match results and analysis
items.description: ESPN breaks down all the action from Night 2 of WrestleMania 42 in Las Vegas.

items.headline: Cardinals top Astros 7-5 in 10 innings to extend win streak to 5 games
items.description: — Masyn Winn cleared the bases with a double down the third base line, and the St. Louis Cardinals won their fifth straight game, beating the Houston Astros 7-5 in 10 innings on Sunday.
